title: crambe scaberrima
intro: Crambe scaberrima, also known as rough sea kale, is a perennial plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its edible leaves and stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. It is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Crambe scaberrima can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and its leaves and stems can also be eaten raw or cooked as a nutritious addition to various dishes.
